Traffic Pros Monthly Featuring Dan Rose
Jon: 	Welcome everyone to this month’s edition of 3X Traffic Pros Monthly; I’m Jon
Benson from 3X VSL.com. And 3X VSL.com/traffic, if you are listening to this and
you want to get in on the monthly action.
I interview the top guys in the world on traffic, from free traffic to cheap traffic to
paid traffic from every medium you can think of. It’s all focused on getting traffic to
videos, video sales letters in particular. So its right up our alley here are 3XVSL.com.
These guys have mastered YouTube traffic. Dan is the guy I am interviewing today.
Dan Rose is probably the best guy in the world for YouTube traffic that I know of.
His partner Mike Chang and Dan have taken their views on YouTube to well over 200
million views. And almost 2 million subscribers now Dan is that correct?
Dan: 	2.3 million between all the channels.
Jon: 	Yeah 2.3million subscribers. This is a huge list and obviously a list that they monetize
very well. I have been out to Austin, Texas where they are located many times and
hung out with them. These guys are really rock solid dudes, and they have got a great
business.
They went from working literally; you tell the story Dan, because it is part of what we
do is inspire people, to let them know that anyone can do this stuff.
I remember them telling they were like 3 of them living in one apartment at one time,
different guys, in different room and doing stuff. Now they have got this office where
they have got 30 people, and call center and everything.
That all got started by Dan really focusing on YouTube traffic and in so doing, and
this is the point I really want to drive home for people today, we are going to be
sharing this.
This is one of the best free traffic strategies that I know of for getting Google to be
okay with you having a video sale letter and driving traffic directly to it.
You guys drive traffic directly to your video sales letter, correct?
Dan: 	Right we also do some ads going through our YouTube channel, but we do a lot of ads
going straight to the VSL.

Jon: 	Right, then that was not possible without some of the tricks that Dan is going to be

in bed together.
So this is a secret that very few people know about. If you have a lot of, you can

Dan: 	It’s actually been a pretty crazy journey. We got started I was about 3 years ago,
where I was running another online business at the time that wasn’t too successful. I
met this guy Mike Chang; he was a friend of a friend. He was saying like I have to
meet this guy and like he’s the best trainer and I’m like, whatever I know have to
train.
But anyways I did one of this guy’s workouts and it was actually really good. So I
actually got in pretty good shape myself using Mike Chang advice, and I thought that,
there is going to be demand for this. I think if it was online that people were going to
buy it.
What we did is we were checking out some videos on YouTube one day. We were at
this party, and we were kind of drunk, and we were just like kind of horsing around
on YouTube.
Traffic Pros Monthly | Dan Rose | 3XVSL.com/traffic 	Page 3 of 27

We found that video about six pack abs were getting crazy views. Like dumb videos
were getting like millions and millions of views. We thought we can probably make
some better videos about getting six pack abs.
So those really how it all got started. Michael was crashed in my place when we first
started this. We had hardly any money. The way we financed everything was by
spending all our savings, and by maxing out on all the credit cards I could get.
Then we got started and we’re making like cheap little video the Flip cam, like fairly
low production budget to get started with. Our advertisement budget was really low.
But basically what we were to do was to make it profitable on a very small spending
only a few hundred dollars a month at first. Then that money wherever we reinvested
it in more ads, scaling up and continually scaling up from there.
We were able to build a really big business out of it. Today we have about 30

way that we did was by having a VSL; I was even testing on the VSL and by doing

Dan: 	Yeah I would say, we can talk a lot about the tactical kind of aspect of the advertising,

Then from there expand from a bigger niche such as the overall fitness market. I think

These strategies are really important for me and I think they’re going to be really
important for you guys as well.
Where would someone start, if they just dove in, a lot of people are they have no idea
where to start when it comes down to driving traffic to their video sales letter. Where
would they start using YouTube? Where did you start and what do you recommend
for other people?
Dan: 	I think in the beginning you do have to go through a phase of, it seems like you are
just like spending a lot of time randomly browsing, but just going through and seeing
like what type of content in your niche is popular on YouTube.
For example I can show you like a few channels that we checked out when we first
got started. I remember like one guy that we both know Vince Del Monte. He’s was
one channel that we checked out when we were first getting started. We saw like there
were a lot of videos he put up that really didn’t take off. There were a few that got
like really, really popular.
One I guess like shortcut that I would say if you’re trying to find a niche is go the few
more popular channels in your niche, and to see what they put up that was the most
popular thing.
From Vince’s stuff you can see the first video is about Six Pack Abs, second one
Losing Belly Fat, Six Pack Abs. You kind of get the impression this is well we got the
impression that the thing that, the niche within all this content that people really care
the most about.
Let me see if I can give you guys another example.
These are the Hodge Twins; they are the 2nd most popular fitness channel on YouTube
right now. With their videos the biggest thing you notice is that if you have seen it in
their videos, most of their videos are kind of like webcam style where it’s just to their
faces.
But a lot of these videos on the top of their page, you can see that they have their
shirts off. Later on we found out too that was a really big key to getting a lot of traffic
in the body building niche.
I would say like just go through maybe the top 5 or 10 channels in your market, and
do this thing where you go to the videos page right here, and then sort by most
popular and just see what comes up at the top.

It may not always be the best ideas, but like if you see something popping up at the

-- 8 of 28 --

Dan: 	Yeah, pretty much everyone bought through the video sales letter.
Jon: 	First of all this is another example of a VSL making a lot of money. What's very
interesting about this is the fact that the majority of your traffic came from YouTube.
At first it was all free traffic, I mean it was all like basically cheap like $300 a month
that was really ridiculously cheap traffic.
How did you build up? Where does someone get started when they want to do this?
Let’s say that now they have got a niche, they have got a couple of videos they want
to upload. How do they get started? How do they get the YouTube traffic, and then
drive that to say a video sales letter?
Dan: 	Actually what I can do is I can show you the videos that we’ve just posted in the
beginning. If you go to the videos tab on any channel then you sort from oldest to
newest.
Here you can see not the video we make now, but like the first videos that we made,
there are actually a few that we took down, because they were so bad.
Basically like this the style video that you see in the beginning, that was what we
started off with when we first started.
So what we did and what we recommend that other people do too is put up a YouTube
channel and find 10 sub topics within your niche type people are interested in.
For us what we came up with is Getting six pack abs actual like Ab Exercises, Cardio,
Weight Lifting Losing Belly Fat, Chest, orgasm. We came up with a few more things
like that that within our market that people would be interested in.
So we put up about 10 different videos, and what we saw was that one video which
we actually had to take down just because it had a copyrighted song in the
background. But anyways just one video that we had up, it was bringing in one or two
sales every single day just organically. And we got about 10,000 views.
For us 10,000 views at the time was crazy with no advertising and very small
channel. From there that kind of validated in our mind that there will some demand
for the product.
If you are just starting off that is what I would recommend you. Write down kind of
ten sub topics your niche interested in, close them up and see if you can get at least
10,000 views on one video. If you do there is probably going to be demand for that
subtopic.
Traffic Pros Monthly | Dan Rose | 3XVSL.com/traffic 	Page 8 of 27

Traffic Pros Monthly | Dan Rose | 3XVSL.com/traffic 	Page 9 of 27

-- 10 of 28 --

If you look at our oldest video here, you can see this 9 minute cardio now has 5.6
million views. Back in the time that also had about 10,000 views. We had one other
work out which we now took down, that had about 10,000.
What we did is we just made an advertising account on YouTube, and we started
advertising those two on YouTube, the most popular videos. I would say the way they
do it now is it’s all managed through AdWords now. So what you have to do is you
have to get an ad account.
You should also set up what's called AdWords Conversion Tracking. It’s actually real
simple to set up all you have to do is drop this little snippet of code on your thank you
page , you don't have to need like coding, or be technical to do that. I just set that up
myself to get started with stuff, the conversion tracking.
I believe when we very first got started, we were spending like $30 or $40 a day on
ads and just spending on the two most popular videos. We didn’t have anything really
complicated like basically we just had like two add groups. I chose a few like
keywords to display the adds- on and the ads started showing up.
I could have been doing a lot of stuff at this point, but I just really wanted to keep it
simple, just test and see if we could make money from this.
Jon: 	Right that’s great, let's keep it with the keep it simple. Let's keep it with how you
started because where you are now and how you started. We’re going to get to where
you are now for advanced guys, but where you started I think very interesting to
people who are just getting started, especially you know to me being a traffic buffoon,
I like to learn all this stuff.
How did you advertise the video specifically, you just mentioned keywords of course,
did you buy the add that said, “What” and how did that add drive that to people to
watch that video?
Dan: 	That’s a great question. One thing I find is that people don't really know the
difference between the types of advertising on YouTube and it’s really, really massive
difference especially when you are first starting off.
The type of advertising that we want when we first started and what I recommend
everybody exclusively when they first start is called In- Search Advertising on
YouTube.
Traffic Pros Monthly | Dan Rose | 3XVSL.com/traffic 	Page 10 of 27

-- 11 of 28 --

These aren’t the ads that show up next to a video when you are watching something
else or before the video in the video player. These are the ads when you search in
YouTube like I just did here, that show up in this top yellow box.
The reason why I recommend that everybody gets started within search is that display
advertising on YouTube you are competing against a much bigger pool of advertisers,
so it is much more competitive. So probably as small advertiser is you first start with
start with that you’re probably not going to succeed.
On the other hand it starts because the most important thing is that you are relevant in
the thing the person is searching for. So there is many, many small niches that it’s
tougher for bigger advertisers to compete against you basically.
So if you chose a good niche product in search it should have kind of like a built in
kind of search keyword market that you can dominate.
So you can see one of our ads right here for one of our channels, basically you search
the add show up and the users clicks and they go to your commercial right there. Then
they can click through and go to the site.

So my questions in why do you go to a 4 minute ad versus going directly to the
video? I know the answer, but I want other people to know the answer to that one.
Dan: 	For that type of traffic for YouTube promoted video traffic, you can send it directly to
your website. What you could do is you could upload your VSL to YouTube and send
it to your VSL on YouTube, and that’s actually something that we are about to test

You can get better traffic and send that directly to your website. The advantage of
promoting video traffic is that first of all, it’s going to build your YouTube subscriber
base. Second of all we find that the cost for click getting them to the video is going to
significantly cheaper than for a banner.

based videos; very similar to what you would see people doing in a regular launch,

I would say that you could do launches to YouTube subscribes just like you go do a

The other thing that we do, I don't know if you guys have seen this too, much but we
do what's called re-marketing to the people who watch our YouTube video. We have
lot of complicated stuff going on right now, but basically what it comes down to is
people who watch certain type of videos will see certain types of ads.
Like when you go on other channels on YouTube, the ads will pull you back to watch
a commercial again.
Jon: 	Can you show us an example of that, because I know that you and I have talked about
that at length, and I think that’s a really powerful strategy.
Dan: 	Sure let me think, and see one of those.
Jon: 	Or even how somebody would set it up, not an example of it, because that’s hard to
find but how it would be. How do you set it up? How does that work?
Dan: 	Okay, it is actually pretty simple to set up. What you have to do is you have to go
into AdWords and get what's called the Remarketing Tag, again just a little code
similar to your conversion pixel. You want to drop that remarketing tag on every
gauge on your site.
Then you can go in and create defined rules it’s called, in order to create audience.
For example, one rule we make is anybody who visited SixPacksShortcuts.com, then
you just you start to set duration for how long they will be in the list say like 30 days
or whatever. And then you have what’s called remarketing audience that you can use.
Then what you need to do is add that audience to an Ad group. It will basically follow
the people in that remarketing audience around the internet, and show the ads
whatever they might have to be watching on the internet.
There is also a little more advance where you can remarket to people who have
watched your YouTube videos. If you go what's called AdWords for videos there will
Traffic Pros Monthly | Dan Rose | 3XVSL.com/traffic 	Page 21 of 27

-- 22 of 28 --

be an option to do this and you don't have to put in tags or anything. But basically you
would just have to set up a list where it’s saying, “Anybody watch for a video on your
channel for the past 30 days.”
The same thing you can remarket to these people in the same way.
When you get more advantage you can do things like remarket the people who watch
certain videos like what they would do is remarket it to people who watch diet video
and different ads. You can also do combinations of remarketing and other type of
targeting. Let's say anybody who watched the video and also is watching another
body building video.
There’s more advanced things you can do. But I would say if you are just getting
started, just go to AdWords, set up a remarketing list for your website, set up a
remarketing list for your YouTube channel that should be fine to get started with.

Are there any types of ads to avoid, and are there any secrets to the actual video
themselves. Like look at the screen capture here of the videos, the thumb nails of the
videos, I’ll get it out here in a minute. They are all very specific, and they are not just
picked at random.
Do you have any video tricks whether it be copy or images and any ads that you
suggest people avoid?
Dan: 	So as far as things to avoid, I wouldn’t say there is anything that’s just universally bad
but there are things that are where when you are first getting started that you should
definitely avoid. Let me see if I can find an example of one of these.
So there is two of our ads that popped up on this video. In this screen shot right here,
this basically shows you the ad formats that I would avoid when you first start off.
This ad plain before the video right here is called an in-stream ad. This ad right here
which is in the little yellow box is called the in-displayed ad.
The reason why I would say to avoid this when you’re first starting off is that this
type of advertising is very competitive, because you are competing against a much
bigger pool of advertisers. You should be prepared to lose at least $30,000 or $40,000
before you can make this site that profitable.
On the flip side if you can make it profitable there is huge amount of money much
more in-search than you could make. So basically a very high risk, high reward.
The thing is like when you are first starting off and you’re spending like 1,000 or
2,000 bucks a month, you can easily spend all that in In-search and probably more.
And In-search we found delivers the best ROI, which is most important thing when
you are first getting started with a lot less risk.
So I was saying your goal should be to be running ads like this eventually. But I
would say until you are spending at least like $2,000 to $3,000 a month you can get
all the traffic you need with In-search only.
Jon: 	And most people get those ads in YouTube they can't wait to skip the ad they are
looking at the countdown, just its skip, skip that’s what I do.
So I think that you would really have to have a lot of reach to get those ads to work.
But it’s cool to know that they do work, I’m surprised that they do.

That’s a really helpful tip. As far as like the thumbnails, or as far as the language that
you suggest using for video stuff, obviously the language of the video sales letter is

you title a video for more traffic, you know hits. Have you found any secrets that
work there in thumbnails?
Dan: 	I would say probably the biggest simple short cut people don't use is that same thing
where you just look at the most popular videos in your top channels in your niche and
just observe kind of what they are doing in their thumb nails and their video names.
So if you look at ours right here, you can see kind of the big things that took off. One
big thing that’s been huge for us is before and after picture, and you see this in a lot of
other fitness channels too. We have also found that pictures of Mike with his shirt off
worked really well.
So as far as in other niches, basically it’s got to be something which is both eye
catching and relevant. You can upload it just like outrageous stuff but it’s probably
not going to get you much mileage if it has nothing at all to do with your video, but
something like very eye catching or relevant. I think it’s the best way to go from now.
As far as for video titles what we found is the best way to tell your videos is actually
make a very short and simple video title.
One example of this home Chest And Back Workout, Extreme six pack abs Work Out.
We found that shorter video titles like this overall had been the way to go.
A lot of people the mistake that they make is that they load up their video titles with
lot of keywords it makes them, make them like really long and almost like a SEO
Robot wrote it.
I feel that a really big mistake, because most of the traffic in YouTube doesn’t come
from people searching. Most of the traffic is going to come from related video when
people are watching something else.
So keywords I wouldn’t really worry about that. I would just worry about making like
short simple title and getting like really eye catching relevant thumbnail.
